issue: Indexable trust pages missing from sitemap
evidence: Live sitemap had 96 URLs and omitted privacy, terms, support, and method-boundaries pages that were reachable and indexable.
seo_impact: High: important trust and policy pages have weaker discovery and weaker sitewide legitimacy signals.
fix_recommendation: Update sitemap authority inclusion rules to include approved indexable trust pages sourced from CMS/backend authority.
acceptance_criteria: Approved trust pages appear in sitemap; each returns 200; each is indexable; canonical matches approved locale URL; no draft or 404 URL enters sitemap.
validation_method: Fetch sitemap, parse locs, request each P0 URL, verify status, robots, and canonical.

issue: Clinical and depression pages indexability does not match sitemap inclusion
evidence: Audit found clinical and depression test pages indexable via page metadata and API lookup but absent from sitemap.
seo_impact: High: high-risk health pages need explicit indexability governance and consistent discovery behavior.
fix_recommendation: Make a product and review decision: indexable plus sitemap only after review_completed=true, or noindex plus no sitemap.
acceptance_criteria: Every clinical/depression URL has documented review_required=true; noindex or pending pages are absent from sitemap and footer/global nav; indexable pages have review_completed=true before sitemap inclusion.
validation_method: Compare sitemap inclusion, page robots meta, canonical, review status, and footer/global nav links after decision.

issue: Footer lacks policy, support, and method trust links
evidence: SiteFooter policyLinks was empty and live footer did not expose privacy, terms, support, or method links.
seo_impact: High: sitewide trust pages lack stable internal links, reducing E-E-A-T and user confidence.
fix_recommendation: Add footer links only to P0 allowlist pages: privacy, terms, support, method-boundaries, and approved existing core tests. Do not link holdlist pages.
acceptance_criteria: Footer has no 404 links; approved allowlist trust links are visible in zh and en; refund, science, methodology, reliability, results, and business subpages are not linked in P0.
validation_method: Crawl footer links from home, test hub, article, and high-risk test pages; verify all linked URLs are 200 or approved deterministic redirects.

evidence: /en/charter, /en/brand, /en/foundation, /en/careers, and /en/policies returned 404 while Chinese equivalents existed.
seo_impact: High: broken trust URLs damage English credibility and must not be linked or alternated.
fix_recommendation: Create CMS content_pages with metadata, or remove these URLs from footer, sitemap, and hreflang until content exists.
acceptance_criteria: No public link, sitemap loc, or hreflang alternate points to English trust pages unless they return 200 and have approved CMS metadata.
validation_method: Route status check plus footer and sitemap link extraction.

issue: Bare /help returns 404 while localized help redirects to support
evidence: /zh/help and /en/help redirected to support, but bare /help returned 404.
seo_impact: High: common help entry path is broken and can lose users and crawlers.
fix_recommendation: Use deterministic support redirects for P0; do not use Accept-Language as the SEO critical path.
validation_method: Request /help and localized help/support routes; verify redirects, status, canonical, and sitemap.

issue: Public /results route is blocked by private noindex lookup route
seo_impact: High: result interpretation SEO cannot use the most natural URL while it redirects to a noindex utility.
fix_recommendation: P0 only decides and reserves route ownership: /results is future public hub, /results/lookup is private noindex utility. Public hub content is P1.
acceptance_criteria: /results/lookup remains noindex and absent from sitemap; /results is not linked from footer/global nav during P0 unless P1 CMS/backend authority exists; no frontend result-guide body copy is added.
validation_method: Check route status, robots meta, canonical, sitemap inclusion, and internal links.

issue: Refund policy route lacks approved standalone destination
evidence: /refund and localized refund URLs redirected to support; refund content page API returned 404 during audit.
seo_impact: Medium: purchase trust and post-purchase support are weaker without a clear policy destination, but policy publication requires business/legal confirmation.
fix_recommendation: Keep refund-policy on the holdlist during P0. Create a P1 CMS/legal ticket after business confirms refund policy content.
acceptance_criteria: dependency=business_confirmation; refund-policy is not linked from footer/global nav during P0; P1 publication requires approved refund SLA and eligibility rules.
validation_method: Request refund routes, inspect redirects, verify footer/order links, and confirm CMS metadata before P1 publication.

issue: Root /privacy and /terms return 404 despite localized policy pages
evidence: Root policy URLs returned 404 while /zh/privacy, /en/privacy, /zh/terms, and /en/terms existed.
seo_impact: Medium: users and crawlers often try root legal URLs; 404 weakens policy discoverability.
fix_recommendation: Add permanent deterministic redirects from root policy paths to Chinese default policy URLs.
acceptance_criteria: /privacy -> /zh/privacy and /terms -> /zh/terms; redirect target canonical is correct; localized policies remain in sitemap.
validation_method: Request root and localized policy URLs; verify status and redirect chain length.

issue: Career jobs indexable state is inconsistent with sitemap exclusion
evidence: Career jobs pages were index follow but absent from sitemap in the audit.
seo_impact: Medium: indexable but unsitemapped large surfaces can become orphaned or inconsistently crawled.
fix_recommendation: Decide whether career jobs are ready for sitemap inclusion; otherwise mark noindex until public API and performance readiness are confirmed.
acceptance_criteria: Indexability decision is documented; sitemap and robots state match; no career jobs hub remains indexable but intentionally unsitemapped without record.
validation_method: Compare page robots state, sitemap locs, and public API readiness notes.

issue: Global links may point to pages before CMS authority exists
evidence: The expansion plan contains science, result, refund, and English trust pages that do not all exist yet.
affected_urls_or_files: Footer, header, sitemap, hreflang, test page internal links, article CTAs
seo_impact: High: premature linking can create 404s, thin pages, or frontend content-authority violations.
fix_recommendation: Create a no-link-until-content-exists release gate for all new pages and clusters.
acceptance_criteria: Every new link has verified 200 status, indexability, canonical, locale alternate, and CMS/backend authority; missing pages are listed as holdbacks.
validation_method: Run link inventory before release and compare against approved URL registry.

issue: High-risk clinical and IQ pages lack a formal review gate in the SEO backlog
evidence: The source report marked clinical/depression and IQ pages as needing boundary and review, but this must become an operational gate.
affected_urls_or_files: Clinical/depression tests; IQ test; clinical-boundaries page; iq-test-quality page
seo_impact: High: health and IQ claims can create user safety, legal, and trust risk.
fix_recommendation: Mark review_required=true for high-risk page backlog rows and require approval before indexable expansion or footer/test-page linking.
acceptance_criteria: Clinical and IQ rows have review_required=true; acceptance checklist includes disclaimer, professional boundary, privacy, and no unverified accuracy claims.
validation_method: Inspect page backlog and release checklist; verify high-risk pages include approved review status.

issue: No P0 route-level validation matrix exists for the discovered foundation issues
evidence: The audit identified multiple route, sitemap, footer, and 404 mismatches that need release validation.
affected_urls_or_files: /sitemap.xml; /robots.txt; /help; /results; /privacy; /terms; footer links; clinical and IQ pages
seo_impact: High: fixes can regress without a route-level smoke checklist.
fix_recommendation: Create and run a P0 SEO smoke matrix covering status, robots, canonical, sitemap, footer link source, and review flags.
acceptance_criteria: Smoke matrix is completed for every P0 URL; failures block release or are explicitly documented with owner and date.
validation_method: Use curl or browser checks plus sitemap parsing and footer link extraction.
